remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Symptom recurrence in 70-80% within 6 months of PPI discontinuation in erosive GERD; highly variable in non-erosive disease; ~20% of GERD is nonerosive, where acid suppression is less effective
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Weight loss of >10% reduces symptoms in 65-81% of obese patients; laparoscopic anti-reflux surgery (Nissen fundoplication) achieves long-term symptom control in 85-92% and eliminates daily PPI need in majority; bariatric surgery in obese patients near-eliminates GERD
remission.gap_size: Large for lifestyle/surgical approaches vs standard chronic PPI use
remission.barrier_detail: AGA 2022 guidelines explicitly recommend PPI de-escalation or discontinuation in patients without erosive esophagitis or Barrett's — yet 84 million monthly Medicare PPI claims in 2019 suggest massive overprescription; most patients are never offered surgical or intensive lifestyle options
remission.last_soc_change: 2022 — AGA Clinical Practice Update on PPI de-prescribing (explicit guidance to attempt discontinuation in low-risk patients); 2022 vonoprazan (P-CAB) approved as alternative; PPI-first strategy unchanged since 1989
remission.outdated_treatments: Indefinite daily PPI in uncomplicated, nonerosive GERD (80% of GERD cases do not meet criteria for long-term PPI; 70-80% of GERD patients on PPIs have no documented appropriate indication); H2 blocker + PPI combination (additive acid suppression not demonstrated)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Alginate-based formulations (Gaviscon Advance) as effective as PPI for nonerosive GERD in some RCTs; LINX magnetic sphincter augmentation device (FDA approved 2012, underused); dietary modification (low-acid, low-fat, eliminating triggers) as primary rather than adjunct
remission.frontier_signal: Transoral incisionless fundoplication (TIF) procedure — no incision, outpatient, durable at 5 years; Vonoprazan (P-CAB) for PPI-refractory cases; microbiome-targeted approaches for functional heartburn vs true GERD differentiation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
